text:
Me: Hi, Paula.

Them: Hello. How are you?

Me: I'm doing well. How's your day?

Them: It's really wonderful. It's great outside. I didn't expect it to be cold, so I had to come back in and get my scarf, but it's beautiful. We've hit New York winter. We made it.

Me: Yeah, between the two, I much prefer New York winters to New York summers. I know that's kind of an unpopular opinion. But yeah.

Them: No, I get it. And doesn't it feel festive? As soon as you come out and it's that snappy cold. Doesn't it feel good?

Me: Yeah. I love it. And frankly, you can't escape the heat, right? With the cold, you can bundle up. It's a little bit easier.

Them: Yes, it's true. It can get…

Me: …heat is inescapable.

Them: Yeah, you're right. It can be intolerable. Do you remember how it was that last week of September? It was still boiling. It was the first time I was like, I think I'm ready for a jacket, a puffer, anything. It was just relentless. My sisters and sisters-in-law and I met for our annual girls trip.

Me: Yeah, yeah.

Them: And we went to Rhode Island. It was supposed to be this New England iconic fall trip, and it was so hot. The leaves didn't really change because it was a drought. I was like, oh man, what's happening here anyway?

Me: Oh gosh, yeah. It's unfortunate. My partner works in climate policy. The moment any of that stuff comes up she's always like, you know why that is, right? I'm like, I know.

[Transcript continues with discussion of climate, AI Collective, Careerspan's origin story, Paula's reflection work, Chief, applied AI in New York, human potential, and closing remarks about next steps and follow-up materials.]
